summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 12 warning, 8 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `test/runbook-add-cli-command.test.mjs:21-50` — Schema validation uses raw text regex on YAML content, not a YAML parser; syntactically invalid YAML (bad indentation, duplicate keys, tab chars) passes all assertions while being unparseable at engine runtime — backlog: replace with `yaml.parse()` + structural assertions before the matching engine is built

🟡 `.team/runbooks/add-cli-command.yml:23` — `flow: build-verify` references a runtime constant in `bin/lib/flows.mjs` with no test asserting the named flow exists in the registry; a flow rename silently invalidates all runbooks with no build-time signal — backlog: add a cross-validation test importing the flows registry before the engine dispatches on it

**Architectural assessment:** Builder correctly scoped this to the data layer only — YAML schema + directory scaffolding, no premature engine. The two 🟡 warnings are real debt but pre-engine and non-blocking. They must be resolved before the matching engine reads runbook files.

🟡 `test/runbook-add-cli-command.test.mjs:44` — Pattern field validation is per-field-globally, not per-pattern; a runbook where one pattern omits `weight` passes as long as any other pattern has it — iterate patterns in a loop and assert all three sub-fields present on each entry

🟡 `test/runbook-dir.test.mjs:34` — Asserts source-code ordering of `mkdirSync` calls by string index; tests source layout, not runtime behavior; a valid refactor of `init.mjs` silently breaks the test without breaking functionality — replace with filesystem assertion after `agt init` invocation

Evidence is solid. The runbook file exists, schema is clean (no gold-plated fields after run_2 fix), all 4 tasks have substantive `title` + `hint`, and `artifacts/test-output.txt` confirms 544 pass / 0 fail with the runbook suite explicitly visible at lines 633–647. Both `🟡` findings are test fragility issues — not correctness defects — and neither blocks merge. Eval appended to `.team/features/runbook-system/tasks/task-1/eval.md`.

🟡 Gate output (provided in review brief) — Truncated; stops before the `add-cli-command.yml runbook` suite. The `artifacts/test-output.txt` artifact is complete and shows all 5 runbook tests passing (lines 633–639), resolving the missing-evidence concern from prior runs. Reviewers must read the artifact to confirm — the inline gate output alone is insufficient.

🟡 `test/runbook-add-cli-command.test.mjs:20` — Schema validated via raw-string regex, not YAML parsing; a structurally broken YAML (bad indentation, duplicate keys) passes all 5 assertions as long as field name strings appear in the text; add `YAML.parse()` step before field checks

🟡 `test/runbook-dir.test.mjs:34` — Asserts source-code ordering of `mkdirSync` calls by `src.indexOf()` on the `init.mjs` source string; tests implementation layout, not runtime behavior; a valid refactor (extracting to a helper, reordering) breaks this test without breaking functionality; replace with filesystem integration assertion

The acceptance criterion is met. The file exists at `.team/runbooks/add-cli-command.yml`, contains all required schema fields (`id`, `name`, `patterns`, `minScore`, `tasks`, `flow`), and has exactly 4 tasks with substantive hints. `flow: build-verify` is verified as a live constant in the flow registry. The full test suite (546 tests, 544 pass, 2 skipped, 0 fail) is confirmed in `artifacts/test-output.txt`, with the runbook suite specifically passing at lines 633–647. The two 🟡 warnings are pre-existing backlog items from prior review rounds — neither blocks merge.

🟡 `test/runbook-add-cli-command.test.mjs:20` — schema validated via raw-string regex, not YAML parsing; a structurally broken YAML (bad indentation, duplicate keys) passes all 5 assertions as long as field names appear as text — add `YAML.parse()` roundtrip before assertions; must resolve before runbook engine ships

🟡 `test/runbook-dir.test.mjs:34` — asserts `runbooks` `mkdirSync` appears after `features` `mkdirSync` by text index in `init.mjs` source; tests source code layout, not runtime behavior; a valid refactor breaks the test without breaking functionality — replace with filesystem assertion after `agt init`
Both are carry-forwards from run_2 (pre-existing, not introduced in run_3). No 🔴 findings. Merge is unblocked.
